plan: 1. Initialize the project with `npm init -y` and update `package.json` with project metadata (name: latency-cathedral, version: 0.1.0, private: true, type: module).
2. Install core dependencies: `npm install three` and dev dependencies: `npm install -D typescript vite vitest eslint @eslint/js typescript-eslint playwright @playwright/test @types/three`.
3. Create `tsconfig.json` with strict mode, ES2020 target, ESNext modules, DOM lib, moduleResolution bundler, and paths pointing to `src/`.
4. Create `vite.config.ts` with basic Vite configuration (root, build outDir: dist, server port).
5. Create `vitest.config.ts` referencing the Vite config and setting up test globals and environment.
6. Create `playwright.config.ts` with a basic configuration pointing at the dev server for webServer and a single chromium project.
7. Create `eslint.config.js` (flat config format) with TypeScript-ESLint recommended rules.

8. Add npm scripts to `package.json`: dev (`vite`), build (`tsc && vite build`), preview (`vite preview`), lint (`eslint .`), typecheck (`tsc --noEmit`), test (`vitest run`), test:e2e (`playwright test`).
10. Create `index.html` at project root as the Vite entry point with a `<script type="module" src="/src/main.ts">` tag, a `<canvas id="cathedral">` element, and minimal HTML boilerplate.
11. Create `src/main.ts` with a minimal entry that writes a visible confirmation to the page (e.g., sets document title or appends text to the body) so the default page loads visibly.
13. Create a placeholder probe asset in `public/` (e.g., `public/probe.txt` with a small payload) as required by the project conventions for same-origin probe assets.
14. Run `npm install` and verify it completes without errors.
15. Run `npm run dev` and verify the local server starts and the default page loads.
16. Run `npm run build` and verify a production build is created in `dist/`.
17. Run `npx playwright install chromium` to install browser binaries needed for future E2E tests.
testing_recommendations: Run `npm install` and confirm zero errors and a valid `node_modules/` tree.
Run `npm run dev`, open http://localhost:5173, and confirm the default page renders visible content in the browser.
Run `npm run build` and confirm `dist/` is created with `index.html` and bundled JS assets.
Run `npm run typecheck` and confirm TypeScript compilation succeeds with no errors.
Run `npm run lint` and confirm ESLint runs without errors on the scaffolded files.
Run `npm run test` and confirm Vitest executes (even with zero tests, it should exit cleanly).
Run `npm run preview` and confirm the production build is served and loads correctly.
risks: Version conflicts between Vite, TypeScript, and ESLint flat config — mitigate by using latest stable versions and verifying the lint script runs after install.
Playwright browser binary download may fail or be slow on first install — mitigate by running `npx playwright install chromium` explicitly as a separate step.
TypeScript strict mode may surface type errors in three.js type definitions — mitigate by ensuring @types/three version matches the installed three.js version.
